meta.disclaimer: Model simulation outputs for information and entertainment only — not betting odds or guarantees.
meta.model_note: Probabilities come from simulating the whole tournament many times from current team strength; they update as real results come in.

narration.headline: Belgian Late Show Denies Senegal in Five-Goal Knockout Thriller
narration.body: Belgium secured a dramatic 3-2 victory over Senegal in the Round of 32 after a breathless comeback. Senegal looked poised for a famous win after Habib Diarra opened the scoring in the 25th minute and Ismaïla Sarr doubled the lead in the 51st. However, Belgium's veteran striker Romelu Lukaku pulled one back in the 86th minute before Youri Tielemans equalized in the 89th. Tielemans then struck the decisive blow in the 120th minute of extra time. Despite the scoreline, the matured expected goals (xG) tell a different story: Belgium won the match with an xG of 1.8, while Senegal dominated the performance with an xG of 3.22, showing the African side was far more dangerous than the final result suggests. Tielemans was the standout performer, earning an 8.3 match rating with his two crucial goals. With this victory, Belgium advances to the Round of 16, where they will face the winner of the other tie. For Belgium, this dramatic escape keeps the final World Cup run of their golden generation alive, restoring belief after a stuttering group stage. For Senegal, it is a dignified but heartbreaking end for an aging core that arrived with high hopes of a deep run, only to fall just short in a classic encounter.

narration.headline: Kane Rescues England as Historic DR Congo Run Ends in Drama
narration.body: England survived a major scare to defeat DR Congo 2-1 in the Round of 32, coming from behind to secure their passage. The Leopards took a shock lead in the 7th minute when B. Cipenga converted a pass from Chancel Mbemba, but England captain Harry Kane turned the match around with two late strikes in the 75th and 86th minutes, both assisted by Anthony Gordon. The final scoreline reflected the overall balance of play, as England's matured expected goals of 2.04 to DR Congo's 0.76 confirmed that the Three Lions created the higher-quality chances. Kane was the standout performer, earning a match rating of 8.3 for his decisive two-goal display. With this victory, England advance to the Round of 16, where they will face the winner of the other tie. For England, the win keeps their heavy national expectations alive, continuing a campaign where fans hope to finally end decades of major tournament near-misses. For DR Congo, the defeat brings a dignified end to a historic milestone. Having returned to the World Cup after a 52-year absence, the Leopards exceeded all expectations by reaching the knockout stage for the first time in their history, bringing immense pride and unity to their nation.

narration.headline: Co-Hosts Mexico Face Heavy England Test in Mexico City
narration.body: A perfect group stage has fueled belief that co-hosts Mexico can finally break their historic last-sixteen barrier on home soil. However, our knockout model positions England as the clear favorites with a 67% chance to win this single-elimination tie, leaving Mexico with a 33% chance. The prediction reflects England's rating advantage despite a mixed group stage where they won twice and drew once. Mexico arrive with flawless momentum, having won all four of their matches without conceding a single goal. England have been more workmanlike, keeping two clean sheets but still searching for a fluid attacking identity. This high-stakes clash features a direct battle of in-form goalscorers, as Mexico's Julian Quiñones and England's Harry Kane both enter the match with three goals to their name. For Mexico, the national press frames this as a generation's best shot at redemption, though manager Javier Aguirre has tempered expectations, stating that "what counts is the final placing." Meanwhile, British coverage describes a familiar mix of heavy expectations and anxiety, with Thomas Tuchel insisting "the bigger the games get, the bigger we will get." Watch for whether Mexico's stubborn, unbreached defense can withstand England's star-studded attack in the altitude of Mexico City.

narration.headline: Mexico March Into the Round of 16 After Shutting Out Ecuador
narration.body: Mexico secured their place in the Round of 16 with a clinical 2-0 victory over Ecuador in the Round of 32. The co-hosts took control in the 22nd minute when Julián Quiñones found the net from a Roberto Alvarado assist, before veteran forward Raúl Jiménez doubled the advantage in the 31st minute. While the scoreline suggests a comfortable night, the matured expected goals (xG) tell a tighter story: Mexico finished with 1.02 xG to Ecuador's 0.73, indicating that clinical finishing rather than absolute dominance made the difference. Quiñones was the standout performer, earning an 8.3 match rating after scoring the opener, assisting the second, and creating three key passes. This victory keeps Mexico's home-soil dream alive as they advance to the Round of 16 to face the winner of another tie. For Mexico, the win fuels a hopeful nation that has long been haunted by the round-of-sixteen barrier, keeping them on course for a historic quarter-final run. Ecuador's brave campaign comes to an end; despite their emotional group-stage comeback against Germany, La Tri could not repeat the magic in a hostile atmosphere, concluding their second-ever knockout appearance with dignity.

narration.headline: France Cruise Past Sweden to Reach Round of 16
narration.body: France booked their place in the Round of 16 with a commanding 3-0 victory over Sweden. The breakthrough came just before halftime when Kylian Mbappé found the net in the 45th minute. Bradley Barcola doubled the lead in the 53rd minute, before Mbappé sealed his brace in the 74th minute to put the tie beyond doubt. The final scoreline was a fair reflection of the on-pitch performance, as France's matured expected goals (xG) of 3.16 to Sweden's 0.56 confirmed their complete dominance. Mbappé was the standout performer, earning a superb 9.6 match rating with his two goals and four key passes, while Michael Olise pulled the strings with two assists. With this victory, France advance to the Round of 16, while Sweden's tournament comes to an end. For France, the win keeps their title aspirations alive as they seek to deliver a fitting farewell for manager Didier Deschamps in his final tournament. For Sweden, who exceeded modest pre-tournament expectations by reaching the knockouts under Graham Potter, the defeat marks a dignified end to a resilient campaign that was ultimately hampered by defensive reshuffles.

narration.headline: France's Frightening Attack Meets Paraguay's Defensive Wall in Philadelphia
narration.body: This Round of 16 clash presents a classic tournament contrast: France's free-flowing, star-studded attack going head-to-head with a stubborn Paraguay side built to defend deep and frustrate. According to our knockout model, France are heavy favourites with an 88% chance to win the tie, backed by a flawless group stage where they scored ten goals. Paraguay hold a 12% chance to pull off an upset, having advanced as a best third-placed team by relying on defensive organization to secure two clean sheets. Les Bleus arrive with immense momentum after three straight wins, powered by the tournament's joint-top scorers Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé, who have netted four goals each. Paraguay's return to the knockouts after a 16-year absence has ignited a passionate domestic debate over their ultra-defensive style, but captain Gustavo Gómez rallied the nation, writing, "When a Paraguayan believes, nothing is impossible." Meanwhile, France are playing with the emotional weight of Didier Deschamps' final campaign, though midfielder Adrien Rabiot warned that their attack-heavy shape has left them "somewhat exposed" at times. The key to this single-elimination tie will be whether Paraguay's low block can withstand France's early pressure, or if Mbappé and Dembélé will quickly find a way through.

narration.headline: Haaland Strikes Late as Norway Edge Ivory Coast in Knockout Thriller
narration.body: Norway have advanced to the Round of 16 after a tense 2-1 victory over Ivory Coast in Dallas. Antonio Nusa opened the scoring in the 39th minute following a setup from Martin Odegaard, but the Ivorian side fought back, with Amad Diallo leveling the score in the 74th minute. The decisive blow came in the 86th minute when Erling Haaland found the net from a Patrick Berg assist. The final scoreline reflected the underlying performance, as Norway's matured expected goals of 2.02 to Ivory Coast's 1.11 confirmed their attacking edge. Diallo was the standout performer of the match, earning a 9.2 rating for his tireless work and crucial equalizer. For Norway, this victory represents a massive milestone for their golden generation, keeping their long-awaited tournament run alive. For Ivory Coast, the defeat brings a dignified end to a historic campaign that saw them reach the World Cup knockout rounds for the first time in their history, fulfilling the high expectations of a proud nation.

narration.headline: Heavyweight Ambition Meets a Golden Generation in New York
narration.body: A fascinating clash of styles takes center stage in New York as five-time champions Brazil face a rising Norwegian side in a single-elimination battle. Our knockout model positions Brazil as the favorite with a 61% chance to advance, backed by their superior rating gap and an unbeaten group stage. Norway, holding a 39% win chance, arrive hungry after navigating their group with two victories, though they are still searching for their first clean sheet of the tournament. The match features a spectacular duel between the tournament's joint-top scorers: Brazil's Vinícius Júnior and Norway's Erling Haaland, who have both netted four goals. For Brazil, the stakes are immense; ESPN reports that the country views this campaign as a "win-or-bust" quest to end a 24-year title drought. Meanwhile, Norwegian captain Martin Odegaard told NRK that "it is allowed to dream big, and we are going to go for it" as his nation enjoys its first knockout appearance since 1998. Watch for how Norway's rested stars handle Brazil's fluid, transition-heavy attack in a match where one mistake ends the journey.

narration.headline: Morocco Edge Netherlands on Penalties After Dramatic Monterrey Knockout
narration.body: Morocco have advanced to the Round of 16 after a dramatic 1-1 draw with the Netherlands was decided on penalties, with the Atlas Lions winning the shootout 3-2. Cody Gakpo broke the deadlock for the Dutch in the 72nd minute, but Morocco's Issa Diop equalized in the 90th minute to force extra time. While the scoreline remained level, the matured expected goals (xG) tell a different story of dominance: Morocco accumulated 1.4 xG compared to a mere 0.23 xG for the Netherlands, confirming that the Atlas Lions created the far better opportunities. Dutch goalkeeper Bart Verbruggen was the standout performer of the match, earning an 8.3 rating for keeping his side in the game. Morocco now advance to the Round of 16 to face the winner of the other tie. For Morocco, the victory keeps their ambitious dream alive under new manager Mohamed Ouahbi, who has urged his players to believe they can win the tournament outright. For the Netherlands, the defeat brings a heartbreaking end to a campaign filled with high expectations. The loss is particularly poignant for Gakpo, who chose to remain with the squad following a personal tragedy, a decision that had deeply united the close-knit Dutch team.

narration.headline: A Historic First Knockout Meeting as Canada and Morocco Clash in Houston
narration.body: This single-elimination tie brings together two nations riding waves of historic momentum, with Canada entering their first-ever World Cup knockout match against a formidable Moroccan side. Our knockout model positions Morocco as the clear favourite with a 65% chance to win, backed by their unbeaten group-stage run and deep tournament pedigree. Canada hold a 35% chance to pull off the upset, arriving with two clean sheets from their group campaign. The match features a thrilling duel of in-form goalscorers: Canada's Jonathan David and Morocco's Ismael Saibari have each netted three goals so far. For Canada, the stakes are heightened by a mix of optimism and anxiety; per ESPN, manager Jesse Marsch confirmed captain Alphonso Davies is available, though he pointedly refused to guarantee a start. Meanwhile, Morocco carry immense national expectations under new manager Mohamed Ouahbi, who has guided them seamlessly after taking over just months before the tournament. According to beIN SPORTS, Ouahbi noted that "the players now believe in their ability, and opponents respect Morocco." With no prior head-to-head meetings between these teams, this first encounter will be decided by who handles the intense pressure of the big stage. Watch for Canada's aggressive transition play to test Morocco's reshaped but highly disciplined defensive line.

narration.headline: Paraguay Stun Germany on Penalties After Dramatic Boston Battle
narration.body: Paraguay have pulled off a monumental upset in the Round of 32, defeating Germany 4-3 on penalties after a tense 1-1 draw through extra time. Julio Enciso opened the scoring for Paraguay in the 42nd minute, before Kai Havertz equalized for Germany in the 54th minute to send the match into a grueling scoreless extra period. While the scoreline remained deadlocked, the matured expected goals (xG) tell a story of German dominance, with Germany registering 1.49 xG to Paraguay's 0.42 xG—proving that the South Americans survived a heavy onslaught. Germany's Florian Wirtz was the standout performer on the pitch, earning an 8.3 rating with four key passes and the crucial assist for the equalizer. With this historic shootout victory, Paraguay advance to the Round of 16, where they will face the winner of the other tie. For Paraguay, this triumph represents a massive national milestone, vindicating the belief of captain Gustavo Gomez who rallied his country by stating that "when a Paraguayan believes, nothing is impossible." Conversely, the defeat brings a dignified but painful end to Germany's campaign, leaving a disillusioned squad to face intense domestic scrutiny after failing to meet their football culture's expectation of a deep tournament run.

narration.headline: Brazil Edge Japan in Houston Thriller to Reach Round of 16
narration.body: Brazil secured their place in the Round of 16 with a hard-fought 2-1 victory over Japan, overcoming an early setback to keep their championship ambitions alive. Japan struck first in the 29th minute when K. Sano found the net, but Brazil responded in the second half as Casemiro equalized in the 56th minute before Gabriel Martinelli sealed the dramatic comeback with a 90th-minute winner. While the scoreline remained tight, the matured expected goals data confirmed Brazil's attacking dominance, with our prediction system recording a final xG of 1.69 for Brazil compared to just 0.23 for Japan. Midfielder Casemiro was the standout performer, anchoring the midfield and scoring the crucial equalizer to earn an 8.0 match rating. With this result, Brazil advance to the Round of 16, where they will face the winner of the other tie. For Brazil, the victory keeps their high-stakes campaign on track as they chase a sixth world title under Carlo Ancelotti, satisfying a demanding national expectation for deep tournament progress. Japan's exit brings a dignified end to an impressive, unbeaten run through a difficult group stage, though it leaves their long-standing national ambition of finally breaking past the round of sixteen unresolved for this cycle.

narration.headline: Socceroos Chase History in Dallas Against Unbeaten Egypt
narration.body: Australia stand on the verge of history in Dallas, chasing their first-ever World Cup knockout victory against an Egypt side that has reached this stage for the first time. Our prediction model places the Socceroos as favourites with a 58% chance to win this single-elimination tie, backed by their defensive resilience. Australia ground out two clean sheets in the group stage, while Egypt arrive unbeaten after scoring five goals but failing to keep a single shutout. The sides last met at the World Cup in a scoreless draw, and this clash promises similar tension. Australian coverage highlights a confident camp, with defender Aziz Behich noting that "belief goes through the roof" as they seek to break their knockout drought. Meanwhile, Egyptian fans are riding a wave of pride, though local reports indicate anxiety over captain Mohamed Salah's hamstring strain. Egypt's fiery coach Hossam Hassan remains defiant despite squad injuries, declaring, "I have lions with me, and I'm not afraid of anyone." Watch for Australia's newly configured flanks to test Egypt's reshaped defensive spine in what promises to be a physical, high-stakes battle.

narration.headline: Colombia's High Ambitions Meet Ghana's Resilient Underdog Spirit in Kansas City
narration.body: Colombia enter the Round of 32 riding a wave of buoyant national pride after topping their group unbeaten, while Ghana celebrate reaching the knockout stages for the first time since 2010. Our knockout model heavily favours Colombia with a 92% chance of advancing, reflecting their superior group-stage form and rating advantage. Colombia's head coach Néstor Lorenzo has raised expectations, stating, "Vamos por más y ojalá lleguemos a lo más alto para dejar a Colombia lo más arriba." However, Colombia must sharpen their finishing after a goalless draw with Portugal left players with what midfielder Richard Ríos described as "a bit of bitterness." Ghana, arriving as underdogs with an 8% advancement chance, have built their run on defensive resilience under veteran coach Carlos Queiroz, who insists "the real world championship starts in the next round." Rebuilding through a severe injury crisis that sidelined key stars, the Black Stars have found a new hero in domestic-league goalkeeper Benjamin Asare, who stepped in to record two clean sheets. These sides last met in a scoreless draw during the 2026 group stage, and this single-elimination rematch will be decided by whether Ghana's compact, defensive block can withstand Colombia's fluid, front-foot attacking style.

narration.headline: Holders Meet Fairytale Debutants in Miami David-vs-Goliath Clash
narration.body: The reigning world champions face the ultimate fairytale story in Miami as tournament debutants Cape Verde, the smallest nation by population ever to reach the knockouts, set up a historic single-elimination tie against Argentina. Our knockout model places Argentina as overwhelming favourites with a 96% chance to advance, backed by their perfect group-stage campaign where they scored eight goals and conceded only once. Cape Verde arrive with a 4% chance after a resilient, unbeaten group run of three straight draws, including holding Spain and Saudi Arabia goalless. Argentina's attack is spearheaded by Lionel Messi, who has already scored six goals in this tournament. For Cape Verde, midfielder Deroy Duarte has been the engine holding their shape together. The sides have met once before, fighting to a scoreless draw in their 2026 World Cup group match. Argentina enter under pressure to defend their crown, with national coverage framing this as a stepping stone, though defender Nicolas Otamendi warned, "Cape Verde got the results it got for a reason." Meanwhile, Cape Verde play with absolute freedom, with midfielder Deroy Duarte asking, "So why not?" Watch for Cape Verde's disciplined defensive block trying to stifle Messi's fluid, positionless attack in a classic battle of survival versus supreme quality.

narration.headline: Petković Reunion Adds Emotional Edge to Switzerland and Algeria Knockout Clash
narration.body: This Round of 32 single-elimination tie is defined by a poignant reunion, as Algeria head coach Vladimir Petković faces Switzerland, the national team he managed for seven years. Our knockout model positions the Swiss as favourites with a 69% chance to win the tie, backed by their unbeaten run to top Group B. Algeria, who advanced as a best third-placed team with a 31% chance, must overcome defensive vulnerabilities after a chaotic group stage. Switzerland arrive in buoyant mood following a 2-1 win over Canada, led by breakout 20-year-old forward Johan Manzambi, who has scored three goals this tournament. Algeria counter with captain Riyad Mahrez, who scored twice in their dramatic 3-3 draw with Austria. Swiss coach Murat Yakin remains confident, stating, "I trust my players. We have a seasoned team, and they know how to handle stress very well." Meanwhile, Petković knows his former side's mechanisms, calling them "a solid block" but insisting Algeria will fight to progress. Watch for Switzerland's disciplined midfield trying to stifle Mahrez and Algeria's dangerous transitions in what promises to be a highly tactical battle.

narration.headline: European Champions Face Historic Underdog in Los Angeles Clash
narration.body: A classic style clash takes center stage in the Round of 32 as Spain's possession game meets Austria's intense, physical pressing. Our knockout model positions Spain as heavy favorites with an 86% chance to advance, backed by their flawless group-stage defense where they kept three consecutive clean sheets. Austria, entering their first World Cup knockout match in decades, hold a 14% chance after rescuing their tournament in a chaotic 3-3 draw with Algeria. Spain's attack will rely on Mikel Oyarzabal, who has already struck twice in the tournament, while managing reported injuries to their starting wingers. Spanish coverage suggests the team is expected to win comfortably, though manager Luis de la Fuente warned his squad after their final group match: "It has been a match that has tested us once again, and the team always responds." Austria arrive on an emotional high, but midfielder Marcel Sabitzer was critical of their defensive lapses, calling parts of their last performance "unseriös" and noting there is much to analyze. To pull off a historic upset, Austria must disrupt Spain's rhythm early, while Spain must remain patient to unlock a compact, highly motivated opponent.

narration.headline: Ronaldo and Modrić Meet in Toronto for One Last World Cup Dance
narration.body: This Round of 32 clash in Toronto is defined by a legendary reunion, bringing former Real Madrid teammates Cristiano Ronaldo and Luka Modrić together in what is likely the final World Cup chapter for both icons. Our knockout model positions Portugal as the favorites with a 60% chance to win the tie, reflecting their deep squad quality despite a flat group stage. Croatia hold a 40% chance to advance, with their tournament-hardened core ready to play the role of dangerous underdogs. Portugal arrive unbeaten but searching for cohesion, having followed a dominant win over Uzbekistan with a stagnant goalless draw against Colombia. Croatia rebounded from an opening loss to England by grinding out wins over Panama and Ghana, showing their trademark resilience. History is on Portugal's side, having won four of their nine historical meetings, including a painful extra-time knockout win at Euro 2016. Sourced reports indicate a tense mood in Portugal, where press coverage has called recent displays flat, while Croatia's camp remains defiant. As Croatia captain Luka Modrić noted to CNN Portugal, "Portugal's players are phenomenal, they are a great team," but insisted his side has nothing to fear. Watch for Portugal's star-studded attack to test Croatia's disciplined, veteran-led defensive block in a high-stakes battle of wills.

narration.headline: Golden Generations Collide in Seattle as Belgium and Senegal Meet in the Knockouts
narration.body: This single-elimination tie in Seattle brings together two proud nations whose veteran cores are chasing one final, historic World Cup run. Our knockout model places Belgium as the narrowest of favourites with a 51% chance of advancing, leaving Senegal right on their heels at 49%. Belgium earned their spot by winning Group G, recovering from two opening draws with a resounding 5-1 victory over New Zealand. Senegal, meanwhile, endured a more turbulent path, bouncing back from defeats against France and Norway to thrash Iraq 5-0 and squeeze through as a best third-placed team. The Lions of Teranga carry a potent goal threat in Iliman Sarr, who has already scored three times in the tournament, and Pape Gueye, who enters fresh off a two-goal performance. These sides have met just once before, fighting to a draw in their 2026 group-stage encounter. For Belgium, this tournament represents a final chapter for their celebrated generation, with midfielder Amadou Onana admitting before the draw that he desperately wanted to avoid facing his mother's homeland. Senegal's Pape Bouna Thiaw has kept his squad focused, warning that "even though we scored five goals, there are still improvements to be made." Expect a tactical battle where Belgium's experienced midfield seeks to control the tempo against Senegal's explosive, direct counter-attacks.

narration.headline: Co-Hosts Face Historic Underdogs in High-Stakes Round of 32 Clash
narration.body: This single-elimination tie brings a classic clash of expectations as the United States, riding their best-ever group performance, meet a Bosnia and Herzegovina side playing in their first-ever World Cup knockout round. Our knockout model favors the co-hosts, giving the USA a 75% chance to win and advance, backed by their strong Group D run and superior rating gap. Bosnia and Herzegovina hold a 25% chance to pull off the upset, arriving with a house-money mentality after advancing as a best third-placed team. The Americans enter with momentum after winning two of their three group matches, while Bosnia and Herzegovina clinched their historic spot following a decisive 3-1 win over Qatar. The sides last met in the group stage of this tournament, playing out a scoreless draw. For the USA, manager Mauricio Pochettino has focused on restoring captain Christian Pulisic's fitness, framing this phase as "a final the squad is ready for." Meanwhile, Bosnia and Herzegovina head coach Sergej Barbarez has unburdened his team, stating, "All of this is a bonus for us." Watch for how Bosnia's physically imposing lineup handles the high-pressing style Pochettino has established for the United States.

narration.headline: Heavyweights Meet History Makers in Atlanta
narration.body: A historic first-ever knockout appearance for Congo DR sets up a fascinating clash of expectations against an England side carrying its familiar tournament pressure. Our knockout model positions England as clear favourites with an 86% chance to win, backed by their superior rating gap and an unbeaten group stage. Thomas Tuchel's side arrives with two wins and two clean sheets, though pundits have flagged concerns over their midfield creativity. Congo DR, entering their first World Cup knockout match in history after a 52-year absence, advanced as the best third-placed team following a thrilling 3-1 comeback win over Uzbekistan. The match features a direct battle of in-form goalscorers, with England's Harry Kane and the Leopards' Yoane Wissa both having scored three goals so far. While England topped Group L, the national mood remains a mix of optimism and anxiety, with pundits noting they are still searching for their best XI. For Congo DR, the tournament is already a triumph of unity; captain Chancel Mbemba captured the national pride, stating, "We're still here; the war isn't over yet. But first of all, I want to say thank you, because it's been 52 years." Expect England to dominate possession, while the Leopards look to strike on the counter.

narration.headline: Co-Hosts Mexico Face Resurgent Ecuador in High-Stakes Mexico City Clash
narration.body: A historic, perfect group stage meets a dramatic, tournament-saving comeback as co-hosts Mexico face Ecuador in a single-elimination Round of 32 tie. Our knockout model names Mexico the slight favourite with a 53% chance to win, backed by their flawless Group A run where they took nine points without conceding a single goal. Ecuador, holding a 47% win chance, arrive with massive emotional momentum after rescuing their campaign with a historic 2-1 comeback victory over Germany.
